title: Wave Echo Cave — The Inner Depths

location.name: Wave Echo Cave — Inner Caverns
location.description: Deeper chambers of the ancient mine. Natural caverns connect with worked corridors. The sounds of waves grow louder. Signs of ancient battle — dwarf and orc remains — litter the floors.
location.environment: underground mine, deep caverns, near-total darkness, echoing waves

read_aloud: The tunnels open into vast natural caverns. Stalactites hang from ceilings lost in darkness. The booming of the underground waves is louder here — disorienting, rhythmic, relentless. Ancient mine cart tracks run along the floors, rusted and broken. The deeper you go, the more remains you find — dwarf and orc skeletons locked in combat, centuries old, never buried.

enemies.position: Feeding on old remains in a barracks room

encounters.tactics: Ghouls attempt to paralyze targets (DC 10 CON save) then feast on the paralyzed victim. Elves are immune to the paralysis.

enemies.position: Scattered through a vast natural cavern with a dark underground lake

encounters.tactics: The ghouls lurk in the darkness. They attack from multiple directions, trying to overwhelm and paralyze targets. This is a dangerous fight due to numbers.

enemies.position: Black Spider's elite guards, sleeping or resting in a barracks

encounters.tactics: If alerted, they form a defensive line. If caught sleeping (Stealth DC 12), the party gets a surprise round.

enemies.position: Shambling through an old smelting room with furnaces
Floating above the furnaces — a flaming skull wreathed in green fire
enemies.special: Flameskull — regenerates 1 hour after destruction unless doused in holy water or dispelled. Casts Fireball (8d6 fire, DC 13 DEX save) and Magic Missile.

encounters.tactics: The flameskull opens with Fireball targeting the largest cluster of party members. Zombies shamble toward the nearest target. The flameskull stays at range and uses Shield as a reaction. WARNING: Fireball can easily knock out or kill multiple level 4 characters.

enemies.position: The ghost of a wizard, haunting his former study
enemies.special: Mormesk — a wraith that guards his possessions. Will negotiate if offered something of value. Attacks if anyone touches his chest.

encounters.tactics: Mormesk uses Life Drain (DC 14 CON save, 4d8+3 necrotic, max HP reduced). He is resistant to most damage and immune to necrotic/poison. Can be negotiated with — he wants to be left alone and will let the party pass if they don't touch his things.

loot.location: Mormesk's chest — +1 AC and saving throws
In the smelter room — hidden in debris

skill_checks.success: Hear the ghouls moving in the dark before they attack.
Recognize the flameskull will regenerate — must be doused in holy water or have Dispel Magic cast on its remains.

dm_notes: This is the grind section — resource attrition matters. The flameskull is the most dangerous single encounter in the cave and can TPK a careless party. Warn through environmental cues (scorch marks, burnt remains). Mormesk can be bypassed through diplomacy — reward parties that negotiate. The seven ghouls in the great cavern should feel overwhelming — use the darkness and the echoing waves to build tension. Remind the party that they can rest, but resting in the cave risks random encounters.
